Notes: Excellent!
I really cocked this one up. But the long evening glides were beautiful, so it's one of my favourite flights yet!
ICL #1
Big gaps between (mostly) weak climbs! Tricky.
100th flight, and first trip in my new humble steed.
Battling headwinds 🐢
Chickened out!
Fuck me.
Dead sky at the end. Glad to have fellow competitors today.
Many mistakes! Not used to this 'good weather' nonsense

Found it hard and don't know why. Poor routing decisions probably.

I'll pretend I landed out at OSB as subsequently I decided 'task be damned, I just want to go high.'
I dare not push it any further. Another nice field.
Great until Newmarket, looked very poor beyond. Judged I could slog through the task or dial into my 1500 meeting, not both!
Sucked big time!
Boomerang
Overtook a bunch of folks, but I pushed it too hard...
Should've rejected more thermals!
There was clearly plenty of lift so I deliberately stayed well clear of it.
A little more on par today.
Lap 1.
A bit excruciating in the middle but I refused to accept 1kt climbs for any longer than absolutely necessary.

One of the more demanding tasks I've set myself...
Very difficult around Lyveden, where a rainy trough caused me to divert significantly.  Just short of Chesterfield there was another, with dark curtains of cloud and rain all around - but with the best thermals of the day just south of it.  All the low grovelling took its toll on my speed and energy, and when I got to the Wash and saw another massive cloudless gap with another stripe of dark grot beyond it, I threw in the towel.

HC in the truly magnificent Cambridge Cloud Rally.  Book early for next year!
Ballistic conditions but no water.
Had to remove control point at Upwood to suit ladder rules.
Woefully under-declared
Only took off to test the FLARM.

Conservative as it was my first XC of the year.
Out of sync for most of the first two legs.  Final leg into wind was storming.
Strange dropouts in the flight recording.

I did a silly thing.  Went back to GRL to restart but didn't notice that the line I was crossing was the finish line, not the start line.  So time was about 30 minutes longer than necessary.
Other than that, a fun day out.

A modest task designed to fit the forecast short weather window.  In the event I could have gone further east or even round again - there were strong thermals (probably wave affected) at Gransden until after 4pm.
A rather pedestrian run downwind followed by a much more effective run back.
Lots of gear changes along the way.  My first 300km of the year...

I thought I'd started too early since the cloudbase was slow to rise.  But the day was dying when I got back to GRL, so perhaps not.  
Straightforward apart from a sticky bit around Tibenham.  Run back into wind was excellent.

Strange day.  Forecast said short and good, turned out long and difficult.
Abandoned due to dinner engagement.
Wasn't convinced the task was on at the start so didn't go back to GRL to make a proper start...

I didn't want to start at 1400, but it was forced on me by low, weak thermals until then, conditions improved slowly heading north, then it went blue, but near Sywell a climb to 4500 got me almost on glide.
Slow, took until 1400 for the thermals and cloudbase to get sensible, and then beyond St. Neots blue, however it worked fine.

Some cracking climbs, bit of overdevelopment around Man, but looked good on the second leg, but it wasn't!, maybe wave or warm air advection, still scraped away south of Cranfield, but still needed to top up as base was lowering
Late start , then base quickly rose to >5K, i screwed up at EDG by pushing too hard and had to grovel for a while till i got going again, note.... when the base is 5K to go down to 1000 agl!
You didn't want to be low under the spreadout, managed that twice, but apart from that very good climbs, light wind and a pleasure to be flying!.
Low base untill about 1300 , in the east base crawled up to 4k, with broken climbs
East was best, strong climbs and base reached 6K, spectacular views.
Good in parts, too late at FAK, was total spreadout
Bit like hard work, good climbs when you could centre in them, base eventually 5K.
Lots of spreadout beyond EDG, slowed right down and had to take some very weak climbs untill north of BAN on the way back when it switched on again
So glad we went east west as the streets were stunning, although the cloudbase meant the working band was usually only between 2.5 and 3.3Kft, but at least we didn't have to worry about airspace!.
What a slog, thermals were mostly weak and broken, big blue hole going north and then a struggle to get on final glide.
Took a long time to get a sensible cloudbase, then improved rapidly going East, towards NOW big gaps and some spreadout, couple of good climbs on the way back to get onto FG
Very underset, but as the airfield had been waterlogged glad to have flown, base 4.8K and 5Kt climbs later on
Very good in the vale of York, blued out on the way back, maybe i should have diverted along way inland, but hoped it might be OK on track
One of the best days ever , certainly the best flight this year¡ Good to have Mark back from Australia , let him do a bit of the flying 'cos he needs the practice 😀! Thanks Mark ¡

Well,started as an old man , finished as an even older man !! Made loads of mistakes but battled on ,thinking I've got no chance of beating my mates ?!?! Turns out they came back early and left me all alone out there batting with very broken thermals, 20 knots wind at times, and my own dumb decisions! Managed to get round though ,!! Thanks to Duncan for your help and good company..
Difficult day for us, not sure why really, too tired to figure it out ! Thanks again for your company Mark
Was easy,,, then not easy ,,,,thanks Jonathan for the company and Robert Theil for the task !!
Ruddy good streets , ruddy good climbs , pretty good pilot (Mark Dalton) in the front seat ,, thanks Msrk and thanks Mike for the task !!
Another great day with Mark, great visibility, one or two 7 knot thermals, lovely streets, winner winner !! Thanks Mark, great company !

Not bad considering i had omitted to connect the correct plumbing for the vario ! Apart from that a glorious first flight of the season with fabulous visibility and great thermals which i managed to stumble into! Thanks Oliver Bridgeman for great company and thermalling !
Lovely day out ,interesting sky,reliable lift,all in all a great day out , thanks Duncan for great eysight,great thermalling !!
Thanks Robert Theil for the task. Thanks Valdis for the company. Difficult day, but great fun.

Started off really well ,clag set in at the first turnpoint and continued until the third turnpoint ,,,very good ,then a struggle,then good again thank goodness !! Thanks Jonathan for your help and thanks Robert T for the task !!
Great day ,was forecast blue but was full of good cumulus and great climbs after huntingdon,, good company, thanks Mark !

Ramble 1 of 3... Should have set off into East Anglia but wasn't brave enough to go downwind. Ended up inventing mini-undeclared tasks to add some km's to my so far pathetic years total!! Please let me know if this is not in the spirit of the ladder....
Ramble 2 of 3... the sky started looking brighter so plucked up courage to go down wind. Mind you, not bright enough to head west (to Rushden) for the club 100...
Originally declared Bourne as the northerly waypoint but ran out of time (had to be home at 4) so turned at Peterborough. Hopelessly slow but a lovely day out....

Set off later than planned due other flying activities in the morning. First leg epic fun... then regretted declaring the back to Diss" bit of my task with the high overcast coming in from the west... but was interesting changing tactics. Faffed about too much near Bury St Edmunds before setting for home as the sky had gone very grey and flat. In hindsight should have gone for it as plenty of random bits of lift all the way home..."
A fab day out… made up a task as I went along and pleased to say my second attempt at Denbigh to Conwy South was more successful than the first (big lesson there!)

CAS to BOZ, great fun, BOZ to DIS fun to start with but then took a gamble away from cloud base as it got grey (as forecast). Didn't work. Big thanks to the Cambridge controller who didn't mind me floating about his ATZ... At least I got to fly a glider!

1st XC of the year
Took a while to remember how to find and centre thermals but after that, a fantastic day out...
Late start. Getting used to a new type...
Epic day out... all down to the can do" attitude of Chris Gill and everyone at Denbigh. What a place...

Season: Thanks to Swanwick Mil and RAF Valley ATC for being so patient and letting me above FL195 and cleared up to FL240 (I have visions of them staring at the radar thinking "What ARE they doing up there?")
Now off to buy some warmer socks..."

Notes: Ramble 3 of 3...

Launched from Audley End.
Originally declared SMB GRF ROU Audley End.
Binned the last bit due line of weather/wall of rain running Stansted-Cambridge hence headed towards Duxford after ROU. Landed at AE after the weather had passed...

At last, a day off coincided with some weather. Planned to float about a bit or do the Club 100 until 3rd at the launch point when encouraged (or more accurately bullied :) to do the days task by 899... An excellent day out and proof that every now and then you need a kick to be more ambitious with tasks. Slow to start with and then ran out of skill westbound near Silverstone. After that all good. Great fun seeing the GRL crowd around the task (except seeing 899 going east when I was 15k shy of EDW going west)
Most enjoyable flight of the season.  First time to Hus Bos
Great day out racing Aidan. Turned out way better than forecast. The heat was much more bearable up there!

Definitely need to work on my task setting there was a lot more distance and speed to be extracted from the day. Originally planned DEV O/R but the clouds stopped about 15km short so just turned around and made it up from there. Good learning experiences for my first XC of the year.

Didn’t trust the forecast too much after yesterday’s landout so was slightly underset today. Weather was so good I took another launch once I landed as I there was still plenty of distance left in the day.
Junior Nationals Day 4
Great day out. Lots of fun going round the task with FVV. Slightly underset as we thought it would be blue so climbed away after the finish and did some more distance.
Junior Nationals Day 1

Had a go at the club 100 after completing my first task. Blued out in front and it was getting late so I didn't want to push it so abandoned. Climbed away again after reaching Gransden and floated around at cloud base locally.
Task 2 for today. My first lap of the club 100, probably won’t be my last.
Junior Nationals Day 7
More interesting conditions. Originally declared HUS O/R but then changed task several times whilst airborne creating an interesting route to HUS once I finally went back to the original task.

Another interesting flight. Weather conditions looked decent to start but quickly deteriorated by the time I reached Grafham. It was no longer soarable on the way back to Gransden so I had no choice but to head downwind where I found myself the lowest I'd ever been. Just as I was about to drop the gear, I found a climb. Once out of imminent danger I then had to push back into wind, topping up on height to get on glide.

Finally got my first landout out of the way. Conditions were getting worse on the way north but were still sufficient. As I was coming back south I watched as the top cover cut off the sun and then the clouds stopped working, hence field.
Junior Nationals Day 2
Junior Nationals Day 3
Junior Nationals Day 5

thought the first leg would get us to Conningsby with a 6000' cloud base a westerly wind and streets to take us over the wash ... as the trace shows we had a low grovel in and out but Conningsby very helpful.  The flight around the north Norfolk coast was simply spectacular hence all the bumbling round taking photos and enjoying the experience .. our trace shows 3 circuits of cromer pier for photos .. E1 rounded CRP at a similar time - we don't think he stopped for photos ! Straightforward run home.  main negative issue was Wendy never goes to Cromer without having an ice cream - laters Wendy

Conditions got better as we got out to the West- left with weak climbs and mainly blue and ended up with cumulus, stronger climbs and higher base. But we just lost out on the final 1000' needed to get home safely and turbo'ed 10k out - ran out of day.
Despite that, a really enjoyable challenging flight.  400K this weekend I wasn't expecting. Nice!

Plan A was to task W, but we changed that to N after the occlusion came through and looking at the sky.
Plan B was therefore Newark o/r with a fallback of going as far North as we fancied.
Fallback achieved!
Varied conditions, some good climbs under the dark spreadout, and broken thermals in the sunshine.
Nice fun Bank Hol excursion.

10/10 for task setting - less for execution!
Waiting around to start at 1pm in the hope of higher cloud base, which was probably a mistake.
Didn't manage to get a climb where we had hoped on second leg, which is a pity as after that, run into Brill looked OK, although also mixed sky and spread out.
Still, we know the engine works!

Slow to start at GRL and headed out East lower than we wanted - things got better after about 30mins - higher cloud base and stronger climbs.  

Being consistent, I put us into holes at Bedford on the way out and back - the Duo felt a lot heavier after the 29! The auto-pilot when I wasn't flying worked much better!

Task was in the Oudie, not the LX

expecting a nice day above 4000' - err no
should have gone round the task anticlockwise because we got shot down by sea air at Soham
so headed home
Lovely day...
Terrible start, then faffed about, but it got better

Talked myself out of going East due to fear of sea air coming in, then ended up scrabbling to climb away at Ramsey (possibly from the sea air from the Wash). Glad to get back to GRL, where the conditions were wonderful!
Nice day until mid afternoon when the high cover caught up with us.

Nice day out but a slog going into wind in a little Discus with now water !
 Could'nt make the last turn  as it was in the sea air

Nice day to start the season.
With Matt Waters

Battery hadn't charged and needed to be home by a certain time.
Only uploading this late as Andy Beatty was nagging me :-)

Slow Slow Slow, but nice to have KPG for company from NE of Marham past Mildenhall on the way back.
Derigged before the rain for a change.
Further back in the launch queue than I would have liked but launched just after the shower of gliders when it became thermic again so it worked in my favour,

Light easterly wind, a bit of a struggle to get going, but then everything went smoothly for quite a while. Coming back from the West, I already found it harder to get good climbs,  and when the situation looked a bit dubious towards BSE from Newmarket I abandoned and instead extended a bit towards the West again.

Turned around about 10km short of the coast, because it was looking a bit uncertain. In the end, the return towards Cambridge against the wind turned out to be better than expected, despite worsening spreadout, and once back in the Cambridge area, the sun came through again and it was maybe the best period of the day around 4-5pm.
Started quite tricky and didn’t improve much. I abandoned the task, because progress was just too slow, and the overcast was becoming a problem.

ICL at Dunstable.. Struggled to get high at first and started too late.. Spreadout killed the thermals and I ended up having my first field landing.. Took ages to retrieve the glider due to wet ground, but all ok in the end
Cloud Rally Day 3. Nice flight but a bit slow compared to everyone else!
Gransden Cloud Rally

I can't claim any vario issues, but the glider did have to run on auto-pilot until the real pilot remembered how to fly.
turned out very nice in the end !

Took the first 100K to get used to being back in 18m mode with 4 barrels and decent thermals - very different from the 15m Nationals!
Base up to 6000' - had a couple of 6/7 knot climbs, but often 3/4 knots and hard to find the core.
Generally good running, until after Banbury on the way back, where I avoided the North option due to airspace, but went into the usual soggy area around Towcester and lost time.
But nice to have a normal flight and get back without the engine!
Thanks to M8 for the task

At the A/T launch point I didn't get the memo from the winch launch point about the sneaky modification of the Banbury TP, or the decision to revert the task.  But my way seemed to work OK. A couple of wobbly bits, but otherwise straightforward, although a higher base would have been nicer.  Got going better after Rugby.  I think a 30mins later start would have been more optimal.  But given I wasn't planning to fly, a nice afternoon out!

slow - fast - slow
Left earlier than ideal to get away ahead of the Juniors, so slow to Bedford, then had a great run to Cromer, down wind and also conditions getting going. Norwich very helpful and gave me a clearance back through. However, hit gloom and spread out after that and limped with some weak climbs, until sunshine at Ely gave 5knots and easy run home.
Missed Cromer ice-cream, but ice-cream van at the comp was an appropriate substitute!

Well that was a fun day out!
Think I got the task right and Norwich very helpful with allowing transit through there (under used) airspace.
Pity I didn't have longer to look round at Ipswich E as I used to live in Martlesham.
Great run to Blakeney, with round thermals and 5knots plus - on the second leg, couldn't quite get to 6000' at Tibenham, but nearly. Went into 2nd TP high as was spreading out and sea breeze there as well.  Wasn't sure what the last leg would be like into wind and high cover, but found some good climbs, so all OK.
(pity it's been nearly 6weeks since I last flew this glider due to a business trip away and the bad weather - thankfully remembered how to fly the 29 about half way round!)

Hard work in places, a romp in others.
I resign as task setter, but it did seem appropriate for the day

Cold feet and some spread out 20K in and 30K out of GRM - otherwise, very nice day out!
Nice to have the company of 899, V11, 205 and 28.

nearly didn't fly as the forecasts were all at odds - in the end, very nice day out - some great climbs (6/7 knots) and some places less good, but generally straightforward.
Oxford and Cranfield very helpful
Thanks to 899 for the task

In true 2023 style, the forecasts were uncertain and didn't fully agree, so we decided to go flying any way!
Things were a bit messy on the way out, cloud base and climbs variable, but OK - got better W of Northampton, and generally we had quite an easy time. Nice to have so many GRL pilots doing the same task.
Practice for SA, hopefully I haven't put Mike off!
Cloud rally
CGC Cloud Rally.
CG Cloud Rally. Variable Barrels

Another day that was more difficult than it looked. Pleased to have made it to LIT despite the headwind. Chickened out at Bedford A/F when confronted with a huge blue hole towards NPT, with a brisk tailwind. Possibly the right decision, as it was a struggle to get home the short distance from there.

A day that did not live up to its enticing forecast, woefully overdeveloped after weeks of rain. I went for it anyway, as I badly needed the practice, which provided the first opportunity to use the FES in anger. 10 minutes was enough, then I switched it off, found a climb, and made it home the old fashioned way. Highly enjoyable despite the poor weather.

Representing Cambridge in the Intermediate class on Day 1 of the Dunstable ICL. First launch from Dunstable. First XC of the season. Second flight on type. Hard to believe I finshed the task, and didn't break anything.
First time to Blakeney Point.  Tide was out.  A very pleasant gentle, slow flight.  Too much time spent low down.
More difficult than I anticipated from the forecast, I was lucky to get round the task. Thanks to Norwich for admitting me to their airspace when the only thermal in range was in their zone.
Great to have flown our syndicate glider for the first time this year.  All went well apart from my feet getting cold when too high to count cows.
A good day for the time of year.  I probably launched too early, it was a bit soft, but got better as the day went on.  One or two dead areas to deal with.  A very enjoyabe day.

A bit soft around Littleport but then going West met the first cloud south of Northampton,  Clouds all the way to STW and back until aftyer Milton Keynes where it was mainly blue again.  Much better than I expected.

An interesting day.  Varied beween towering cumulus with good climbs to puffy clouds that looked good but were not.
Some big holes.

I had a plan but the somewhat showery weather had other ideas so it quickly became an undeclared flight. Early-on going up at 2 knots was luxury, then I got into a street and was tearing along at up to 100 kts for 25 minutes before reality reinstated itself approaching Corby.  A huge dead area between me and my declared TP of Skeffington meant that I could just get there, but nowhere else afterwards, so I turned around at EYE.  On the way home another post-shower dead area between me and the club required a cloud climb to get onto final glide.  All in all a rather interesting flight.
A gentle meander admiring the scenery.
Bit claggy at GRM but booming on the way home.
Ballistic on first leg. Next two harder, much helped by two great climbs at Didcot and Bambury. Pleased to see the gang from GRL on the third leg and diverted to say hi.
Challenging! Crud in the middle of the country with poor vis, low cloudbase and nothing to aim for. OK at start, GRM and finish!
Excellent day out with M8 and HA. After an unpromising start, cloudbase went up to 5000'. Should have had water and should have gone on to BUG.
Good fun with most of the usual suspects.

A variable day: some streeting, some spreadout, some 90 degree diversions when low and occasional strong climbs of 5-6 knots. Spreadout slowed the return from Gaydon against the wind, but once back at Saltby the sky looked much more of a classic scattered cumulus gliding day upwind, so pushed on to Hormcastle. Some great cruising and strong climbs on this leg. This surprised me as the wind was coming directly off the North Sea.
Many thanks to my new gliding friends at Saltby for the warm welcome and for making the flight happen.

Nearly pushed offline from the launch queue when a pundit landed back and the sky looked overcast. But I decided to give it a go, and to my surprise got a climb peaking at 7 knots to cloudbase. One of the fastest legs I've ever had to Ipswich East, followed by some climbing straight ahead after the turn. It then got progressively more difficult: gusty thermals with poor averages and I spent most of the afternoon west of the club pushing into wind and being blown back in thermals. I did eventually reach Northampton South, but was soon low again. The wind was strong enough to blow me back to the club in about an hour, so I stayed in weak climbs and it briefly looked like I might climb away and be able to glide back. However, coming up to St Neots I was still low so fired up while I had fields beneath me. Thanks to 899 for the task?

Fun flight with a couple of struggles. First at Wattisham I came off the energy line to make sure I didn't fall into the ATZ. The irony was that I then got ATZ penetration approval when low. By the time I climbed away, my speed had dropped from 116 kph to less than 80 kph. The second was at Naseby which was in a blue hole and more than 20 knots of wind. The rest was OK and the return from Framlingham was epic.

Started very well, but then found it increasingly difficult to get good climbs despite the occasional five knots. This made it a challenge to get to Bedford and back in the blue, finishing at 18:30! Rewarding to get round despite the slow speed.

A slow start with some uncooperative thermals. However it soon improved with some streeting and the cloudbase rose slightly. Near TIB the cumulus became shallow and sparse, and the second leg relied as much on haze caps as cu. After STN there were some better defined clouds briefly which I used to get most of the height needed to get on glide. This turned out to be a good move as by the time I landed at Saltby it was almost completely blue. 
Thanks to Robert Theil for the task. Thanks to all at Saltby who helped me get in the air and winched me directly into a thermal.

Masterclass flight with Steve Eyles (P1) from Nympsfield. I did (most of) legs 1 and 3.
Interesting flight with great views over country that was new to me. Tricky on the last leg, but Steve brought it home despite the ballast in the front.

Launched after the rainy front went through. On the way to Corby there were thick bars of cumulus separated by blue gaps with some top cover - wave? Between Corby and Langar the clouds thinned out and it became climb-and-glide into the strengthening wind which reduced speed. The last section to Sheffield became very strong with good climbs and energy lines. I was very grateful to finally turn downwind and the run back was quite straightforwards, with a lovely long final glide that started 'under' and became increasingly 'over' thanks to being able to route under active cu.

Planned the task to stay in the forecast good area, aligned with the wind in the hope that it would street. Good on the first leg with well connected climbs under a low cloudbase. Trickier on the second exacerbated by some poor decisions including one of my lowest ever saves. Last two legs better, with enough height at Wendling to get back to better air and bring it home under high working cu.
Very nice on the downwind leg.

Just having fun.. Launched at the back of the Junior Nationals grid, just a short task planned to try out some new instrumentation.  Lovely conditions all the way around, no really low points, no rush.
Got back to GRL at 4,000ft and hung around watching the last of the Juniors return from their task.  Once they had cleared the runway, thermalled down with full airbrake and landed. Magical day.
Tricky at first with weak, broken thermals on the way North.  Lovely visibility over the North Sea.  Last bit home was easy...

I am not sure why the declaration did not get onto the trace, as I entered the task into my new LX9000.
But a lovely day for the first X-country of the season, lots of lift including some 8 kt climbs.

late take off after the comp aircraft had completed their aerotow. Came off tow at 2,000 , found thermal locallly got to 3800 and started the task set off to potton mast  with lift on the way.  Once we got to Grafham Water we had enough height to get back at 90 then 100 knots.
Missed Good Friday but enjoyed this as a consolation flight today. Good conditions in task area.
An unexpected day for cross country.
HC flight in the cloud rally
Flying non-comp at Bidford regionals.
Ugg boots 🥾 kept my feet warm. Nice slow flight to the mynd
Great fun, pity I did not load water...
Much too blue for my taste,  abandoned as I had a commitment this evening and I really did not want to land out.
Task 7 Junior Nationals at GRL
Abandoned due to showers. Thanks to Peter Baker for lending me a battery !

First XC of 2023..!! 
Dug myself a few holes... managed to dig myself out of them. When I landed the FES prop wasn't parked so that probably didn't help on the glides. So awful speed.
Great fun day though, flying along with the Juniors. Rekindled my affection for XC soaring!

Once the depressing top-cover cleared it turned into a lovely soaring day. Downwind to FRW was a blast. Upwind towards NOS was OK until things got really soft after GRL. After a very careful inspection of many fields I managed to find the best farmer's field I've ever landed on, plus the farmer was an aviation enthusiast with a meticulously restored Tiger Moth in his fancy hangar 😀

First XC of the year. A lovely day out with some stonking climbs! Cranfield was very helpful as always. I almost threw it all away by cocking up my final glide by being a just a little bit too eager - every day's a school day…
Difficult day - partly blue - but I managed to get round!
Difficult start due to fragmented thermals and strong wind, Things improved. Evidently I was the only Funster to complete this Cloud Rally task!
First x-country of the season.
Left a thermal too early and messed up from there onwards.
Junior Nationals 2023 Day 6
Junior Nationals 2023 Day 4
Cambridge Cloud Rally Funster Day 3A
Junior Nationals 2023 Day 7
Cambridge Cloud Rally Funster Day 2A
Cambridge Cloud Rally Funster Day 4A
Junior Nationals Day 2
Cambridge Cloud Rally Funster Day 1A
Junior Nationals 2023 Day 5

Dicked around at Cambridge on the way out, I know not why.
Need longer wings.
Great Day! Towed this morning and flew the first task of the season in K8
Leicester was very soft when I got there, and there was a big, slow diversion
Very slow, mainly due to digging myself a big hole on the third leg, and only just getting out of it.  But a fun, challenging flight.

Absolutely storming in East Anglia.  Unfortunately there was a very large spreadout between GRL and NOS.  I got through it OK, but had very few options on the way back.  Was hoping for some slightly better climbs underneath it, but fell out 20k before home.

It was all going well, then I decided to play safe on the final glide, and diverted to a good looking cumulus.  Unfortunately, all I found was heavy sink, and I ended up at 1100 feet grovelling, found a weak but developing climb, and limped home late.
First UK XC flight of the year.  Somewhat underset.  Mostly straightforward - though a little spreadout near Great Malvern.
Lovely day, and could have done the declared task, but needed to get home earlier than that.
Fun flight off the back of the Juniors grid.  Good conditions, though a bit of spreadout at the end.
Slow, but a good use of the available day.  With 899 and V11
A very slow club 100 - lots of spreadout.  Some good climbs, but hard to find.
Gorgeous day.  Could have been much longer, but out for dinner!
Good for later September.  Even a bit underset - but tricky in parts.
A fun flight with a mixture of stonking and soft. Wave influence caused a problems.

Low cloud-base for much of the flight. A few decent climbs but lots of soft areas where caution was needed. With 899 and 871. 700 did task the other way round as we failed to advise Wendy of our changed plan.

Task set by Robert (899) seemed spot on for the day. West of TOW looked very spread-out, as did north of BOS. Lakenheath advised they had some high speed descents in their ATZ, so I had to back track to get enough height to cross their MATZ and lost about 20 minutes. Some good climbs mixed  with some very soft areas. A fun day. Controllers very helpful as usual.

Very weak and low at GRL. Started at about 2200ft above GRL in the blue. Very difficult for the first 40 minutes then good conditions until HUS when it became weak again. Had a reasonable climb to cloud-base north of Sywell but needed another climb to get home. At Little Staughton a mini stubble fire triggered a thermal but there were trucks nearby and I suspect the fire was being hosed out. Still not enough height as the thermal stopped but managed to find a climb at St Neots. Just pleased to get round.
Started early to get clear before the Juniors launched. Didn't get a good climb until after turning Bungay when conditions improved. A mix of lovely streets and soft areas.

A good run to Blakeney Point with lovely round thermals. Very pretty on the coast. Struggled a bit going round the Norwich Class D as I wanted to keep well away and not get low and risk drifting into their airspace. Once clear of Norwich I found good climbs. See breeze almost into Ipswich turn point. Cautious on last leg as high cover had come in as forecast but still OK climbs. A super day out.
Another good day. Well worth a winch launch. Should have put water in.
A long wait for thermals to start., then a high cloud base with good climbs except low down.

A fun day out with 700, 841, 899,W8 and 871. Very soft in places but also some good climbs. Turned Broadway at a decent height then there was a blue gap and in the distance what looked like a decent energy line on track. Unfortunately none of the clouds delivered so diverted to Banbury hoping that the house thermal there would work. Had a close look at the engine switch approaching Banbury. The house thermal was working well and peaked at over 7kts. The last 50 k was very soft but found a good climb at Bedford which was not really needed. Thanks for the task Wendy (700).
First cross country for quite a while. Slow compared against Andy, Peter and Robert but I just needed to fly
Started early with low cloud-base as spread-out was forecast. Some spread-out towards the end of the flight. A better day than I expected.
A difficult day. Clouds looked good but few delivered. Some good cores but hard to find and to centre in. Still, good to be in the air. Lakenheath and Cambridge controllers very helpful as usual.

First flight in M8 this year. High cover and weak thermals at GRL. Set off at about 2000 feet and headed west as the sky looked much better in the west. Conditions gradually improved with cloud base about 6000 feet at GRM. Soft in places and good thermals hard to find. A fun day to start the season.

Had a 4kt climb  after the winch launch, took it cloud-base and started immediately. Good run to south of Oxford, then very soft around Didcot. Soft on the last 50k to LEI. Found the day quite difficult as most clouds didn't deliver much.

SS suggested best in the east so planned task mainly to the east and hoped to make use of streeting. The only good street was from GRL to Mildenhall area. Apart from that is was mainly climb and glide. Some very soft areas with the odd booming bit. The sky was blue with s few haze caps on the last leg with very weak climbs. Happy to get round. Controllers at Lakenheath and Cambridge very helpful as usual.

rinse out day for 72 ... first we fell out of the sky when trying to climb away from 1400' when the electrics turned off ! - so we re-lit and  explored the undeclared limits of the day - we met sea air at Feltwell and again at Thetford !! we also had a close runway inspection at Lakenheath (128.900) and just for good measure we went downwind to Sackville to finish with a tricky get back against a 20 kt headwind' Wendy got the prize for best thermal of the day (7.1 kts) - sort of fun

amazing what passes for a good day this season so far !
downwind first worked out, but the sky had not improved for the 95k slog into wind - rarely got above 3000' and had lots of low save practice. Perked up quite nicely for the last 70k  Big shout out to amazing Cranfield ATC who saved my bacon by letting me fly all over their ATZ while I was scraping out of the weeds - they held traffic on the runway briefly and changed departing instructions to go round me !
mostly 2-3.5 kts to 3200' best bits in EA 4-5 kts to 4000'
Nice day out with Charlie Brooker

right task but not quite as straightforward as I expected 
grovel grovel ... boom !
Better than yesterday!
The sky to the west didn’t look very inviting so the consensus was to go north where the sunshine was when we launched.  Some good climbs but mainly overcast north of Rutland.  With 72, 871 and M8.

Junior Nationals task lap 1
Flew dry which was a mistake.
Junior Nationals task lap 2
Low and slow to Northampton, then spreadout until after Peterborough.  Booming to Blakeney Point and after that mainly difficult climbs until Newmarket when the weather switched on properly.
Nice to get a flight in end of September.  A few bits of spread out but otherwise very good conditions.
Quite difficult until I got to MK when it turned into a gliding day.  Thanks to VII for the task.
Not so good at GRL, once I managed to leave it improved.  Later some gaps and spread out but an easy ride home.
A short task for a short weather window.  Thanks to VII for persuading me to fly.

Very good until Marham, a bit soft near Fakenham.  Good again to abeam Bury but then 8/8 to Wormingford.  Lukily the sea breeze front was not far and a climb there got me to the turnpoint.  Went back to the front which gave me enough height to glide towards a little sunshine.  A satisfying flight.
Underset the main task so did the club 100K to finish a great day.
With M8 and 871.  Mainly difficult with little sun on the ground until we got near Lakenheath when the weather finally switched on.
This was hard, not sure I enjoyed that. Not much sun on the ground for most of the flight.
Low and soft to start with but the occasional good climb later.  Thanks to 205 for the task.
Low and blue to start with, cu up north with high cloud base, blue again on final glide.  An easy day out, thanks to Mark Dalton for the task.
Very good conditions.  If only I could forecast it.
A bit soft at Didcot but apart from that a well set task!
With M8 and 205.  Low to start with, very good in the east, finished just before the spread out became too extensive.
Reduced the task a bit due to approaching top cover.  A silly mistake near Banbury slowed me down.
A slow start and lowish base but it is the middle of September.
Nice task by M8, very soft until HUS but nice conditions on the last leg.
Much better than anticipated.  Blue to start with and HusBos was in a big blue hole but otherwise glorious cumulus.  Did the club 100K after the main task.
Yes, 150K at 56kph in July 🤣.  Big showers at the first turnpoint, the 2nd in dead air after a shower.  Glad KPG persuaded me to fly though.
52kph in August.....  I was the only one flying xc from GRL today, this should have been a clue!
Fun flight with 205, M8 and 700 who set the task.
Started even earlier than usual to avoid the Juniors.  Soft and low until Cromer, nice streeting and higher on the last leg.
It was going so well until spread out slowed me down around Great Malvern.
This was difficult, glad to get round though.  Thanks to 700 for the task.
Another great task from M8.  Every day in these easterly winds seems different.
Fab day out with 841, 871, M8 and 205.  Thanks to M8 for the task.

Low and tricky to BSE but then somebody switched on the weather.  Some streeting and difficult to find cores. 
Sadly had to fly empty.  Thanks to Phil A for the task.
With the cloud spreading out and hazy visibility I decided to turn for home at Silverstone.
A very good day for my first flight in 899 this year.  I could have been faster if I had connected my vario speaker, D'oh!
Difficult going west of Northampton. Lots of things that looks like cumulous clouds, but apparently weren't formed by thermals.

This was supposed to be a quick sprint round a short task within a forecast patch of very good conditions across East Anglia, and did indeed start well. However, the top cover never cleared east of Bury St Edmunds, so despite a good air mass, climbs were hard to find going into Framlingham. Ran a convergence that was probably a sea breeze front NE from Mendlesham to get close to the TP, then darted across the dead air to FRW, back to run the convergence SW for a few more miles, then a long glide under a dead sky to finally find another climb near Hinderclay Meadows. After that, all pretty straighforward.
Cambridge Cloud Rally task.
Cambridge Cloud Rally Task
Conditions a bit difficult around Gloucester, but otherwise a romp.
Only time for a quick 150 km before an early evening engagement. RASP's forecast was pretty much on the money.

Late take-off (1pm) due to commitments in the morning. I thought I had bitten off too much with the impending high cover and early finish. Generally good conditions, but I found thermals hard to core on second leg. Best climbs happened to be just before each turn point. Climbed to 6100' for final glide with very good energy all the way home.
Flew like a Muppet today :-(

Good task for the day - thanks to M8 (Mike Smith). Slightly distracted by non-starting engine that put me in a field last time out.
Mr Solo started both before task and after finish, so I live in hope that he now knows what is expected  :-)
Variable conditions around the task today : blue to MK, then increasingly good to AND and back up to BIC and to BRO. More difficult coming home beyond Silverstone.
A fun flight to the Mynd with JR. Excellent climbs around even in the spread-out. Softer in/out of the Mynd.

What a day! Didn't really have much expectation today due to the grey sky but Chris Gill was in the air before 7am and told us it was waving when he came back so off I went. Got as high as 9500' near Abergele and tried to push into Conwy Valley but not much luck so I came back. It was a surreal feeling - everything was so peaceful and smooth in the wave. Absolutely loved the experience and can't wait to do it again! Thanks Chris for the advice over radio and Andy for the tow!

wow that was hard work. First XC of the year (very belated!) and I thought I forgot how to soar. The first 2.5 hrs were spent struggling with broken thermals, until suddenly the clouds became more reliable. So, thinking all the other people must have made around their tasks by this point, I still went to Littleport in strong head wind, and managed to catch the last cloud lingering above it just before the sea air came in. Didn't complete the task but that was good fun.

Well I was warned by the pundits that west of A1 would be bad after 1pm, and guess where I went and ended up landing out...Everything was going so well until I crossed the A1 from Rattlesden, and it all went downhill from there. Managed to take a climb to cloudbase from 1300' right next to Lyvden, but there was a massive grey hole towards Gransden that I knew I would have no chance getting home. With the field conditions in June, I decided to turn back to Lyvden and landed there. Thanks to Mark, the CFI at Lyvden, for providing great company and running the wing. Also to Rob Bryce-Smith for towing me out. I definitely learned a lesson here...
Cloud Rally Day 3. Finally a completed task for this season! Great fun.
